entry.title: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content:
Hi, 

so we have set up SAP Logistics Management and now want to try the Android and iOS Apps but we are running into serveral issues. 
First of all, we are using the "mobile Services" and not Work Zone, which shouldn't be an issue as I understand. 
Problem with iOS:
When opening the App SAP Warehouse Logistics I have to accept a licence agreement, when done so, I have to scan a QR Code. So far so good. Now I scan the QR Code and I'm getting a success notification which I confirm. After confirming the app sends me back to accepting the license agreement. So I'm caught in a loop and can't connect to SAP LGM. 
App Version: 1.1.0 --> no Update available
iOS Version: 26.3
Problem with Android:
After scanning the QR Code I can connect to SAP LGM and set up a PIN Code. After that I have to select a warehouse, but that is not possible. The app says "An error occured." There is a Button "try again" but when ein click it nothing happens. 
App Version: 1.1.0 --> no update available
Android Version: 13

Anyone else has the same problem? My guess is, that the problem is somehow connected to SAP Build Work Zone but I'm not sure why I should need it, as we use the Mobile Service.

entry.title: Connecting SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX - A step by step guide
entry.content:
This guide explains the clean, correct setup for integrating SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX. It covers both:

  • Landscape Discovery

  • Project Synchronization

Prerequisites

Before setting up the integration, make sure you have the following:

  • Admin rights in SAP LeanIX

  • Global administrator access to an SAP BTP cockpit that has the Cloud ALM API registered

  • S-user with the role Edit Cloud Data on SAP for Me.

    • You can manage your users and authorizations by following the steps described in Manage User Authorizations.

    • You're assigned to the customer number.

 

Step 1: Provision SAP Cloud ALM

  1. Request SAP Cloud ALM via SAP for Me
    Find a how to guide here: Requesting SAP CALM
  2. Setup SAP Cloud ALM
    Find a how to guide here: Required Setup for SAP Cloud ALM

Do not continue until this is confirmed working.


Step 2: Create the Service Key in SAP BTP

  1. Open the BTP subaccount hosting Cloud ALM.

  2. Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions and click create.

  3. Under Basic Info, provide the following details:
    • Service: SAP Cloud ALM API

    • Plan: standard

    • Runtime Environment: Other

    • Instance Name: Enter a meaningful instance name.

      The length of the instance name must not exceed 32 characters. Use only alphanumeric characters, numbers from 0 to 9, periods, and hyphens. Instead of spaces, use underscores.

  4. Choose Next
  5. Add the scopes
    To add the scopes, insert the following JSON and replace the relevant parameters with your instance name and the required scopes, respectively. The instance name must be the same one that you entered under Basic Info, in step 3.

    {
    "xs-security": {
    "xsappname": "<your-instance-name>",
    "authorities": [
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.landscape.read",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.calm-api.subscriptions.read",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.ops-im-display-ui",
    "$XSMASTERAPPNAME.ops-im-configure-ui"
    ],
    "oauth2-configuration": {
    "credential-types": [
    "binding-secret"
    ]
    }
    }
    }

  6. Choose Create

Create Bindings

  1. Next to your instance, choose (Actions) and select Create Binding.

  2. Enter a name for your binding.
  3. Choose Create
  4. Next to the created binding, choose Actions and select View.

Result:

You can now see your service credentials in JSON format.

Important: You only need one service key, even if you configure both Discovery and Project Sync.

That’s all required on the BTP side.

For more information, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/integrating-sap-leanix?locale=en-US


Step 3: Configure the Integration in LeanIX

In SAP LeanIX go to:

Administration → Integrations

You will configure two integrations separately, but both use the same service key JSON.


3.1 SAP Landscape Discovery

In the administration area of SAP LeanIX, navigate to Integrations.

    1. Choose Add Integrations
      .

    2. Locate SAP Landscape Discovery and choose Configure.

    3. Under Select Capabilities, select the primary capabilities that you want the integration to discover. This determines which SAP services and systems to include in the discovery process.

       

      Optional: Under Additional Capabilities, enable optional capabilities such as SAP system-level information and discovered flows. For more information, see Additional Capabilities.

    4. Copy the service key from the SAP BTP cockpit that you created in step 2 and paste it under Authenticate via API.

    5. Choose Save.

Once active, discovered SAP systems and services will appear in the Discovery Inbox for mapping to fact sheets.

To learn how to use the SAP Discovery inbox, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-landscape-discovery-inbox?locale=en-US


3.2 SAP Cloud ALM Project Sync

  1. In the administration area of SAP LeanIX, navigate to the Integrations section.

  2. Click Add Integrations.

  3. Locate SAP Cloud ALM for Managing Projects and click Configure.

  4. Download the service key from the SAP BTP cockpit that you previously created and input it under Authenticate via API.

  5. Click Save.

For more information and troubleshooting please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/configuring-sap-cloud-alm-integration-for-managing-projects?locale=en-US

What to expect:

  • Projects from Cloud ALM can synchronize to LeanIX

  • Updates from Cloud ALM flow into LeanIX

For more information, please refer to the documentation on SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-discovery-configuring-cloud-alm?locale=en-US


Result

After completing this setup, SAP LeanIX and SAP Cloud ALM operate as a connected system rather than isolated tools.

Your architecture repository reflects real SAP landscape data through automated discovery. Projects running in Cloud ALM become visible in LeanIX, creating transparency between transformation planning and execution. Stakeholders can trace initiatives, applications, and systems across the entire lifecycle.

The outcome is simple but powerful:

  • Architecture decisions are based on actual system data

  • Transformation roadmaps align with active projects

  • Execution progress feeds back into strategic planning

Instead of managing architecture and delivery in parallel worlds, you establish an integrated SAP toolchain that connects strategy, planning, and operations end-to-end.

entry.title: Re: Develop custom report in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @harshita_k10a126,

Point 1.

Option A: Embedded Analytics

Use this when you need KPIs, pivots, analytical lists, drilldowns:

  • Custom CDS Views
  • Custom Analytical Queries / Custom Analytical Queries and Services
  • Optional is Publish to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) or create a Multi-Dimensional Report from the query

SAP positions Custom CDS Views and Custom Analytical Queries exactly for this scenario

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/e30de6eae4d24d70b65996ac8ff88848.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/33f3a229ecfe438ca6b2053c48b4e1bb.html

Option B: Developer Extensibility ABAP Cloud/RAP

Use this if you need:

  • a fully custom UI/flow (custom selection logic, validations, actions)
  • complex logic that can’t be modeled in analytics
  • a custom OData service / Fiori elements app you control

This is SAP Developer Extensibility approach for Public Cloud

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e3527dfa1c5a41f69d4e63691f174f0a.html

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4036/4243/?utm_source=chatgpt.com&tab=overview

Point 2. Embedded Analytics step-by-step

Step 0: Check if SAP already delivers what you need

1. Open View Browser to explore CDS views/fields

2. Open Query Browser to search standard analytical queries

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2170')/S35

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1068')/S36

Step 1: Ensure the fields you need exist in the analytics sources

If you’re missing fields:

  • Add them via Custom Fields / in-app extensibility
  • Then make them available for reporting via Data Source Extensions this is the intended mechanism to retrieve additional fields SAP-delivered or custom fields into enabled data sources

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/52b845ed9a2e45b8bd556fc395340ad2.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/f71b07567568414face7043990335367.html

Step 2: Build your data source with Custom CDS Views

1. Open Custom CDS Views

2. Create a new view

3. Add fields/associations and follow modeling rules (keys, currency/unit semantics, correct joins

4. Save and publish

Optional (only if needed): You can also expose a Custom CDS View as an external API useful for consumption outside SAP

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b0000072fe994d7b9d5e015adf066059.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/0f2be3d26b7f48b0b0c426c188b55a89.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/ed003551d9c8456b914f7c94bd311b8a.html

Step 3: Create the report with Custom Analytical Queries

1. Open Custom Analytical Queries

2. Create a new query using your CDS view or copy an existing query and adjust

3. Define:

  • dimensions, measures
  • filters/parameters
  • calculated / restricted measures

4. Use Preview to validate results

Optional (very useful) enable runtime extensibility so business users can add additional fields at runtime without rebuilding the query

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/33f3a229ecfe438ca6b2053c48b4e1bb.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/4ef099d254cd4f8a96df001496f53952.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/25ed791bbae342b1bae8451744838cad.html

Step 4: Deliver it to users

In Public Cloud you typically don’t create brand-new catalogs you extend predelivered catalogs using Custom Catalog Extensions then assign roles. SAP documents this as the standard method

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/a58a58cf98584af6b4b1e6081b1cc6aa.html

Step 5: Transport DEV ->QAS ->PRD

Use:

  • Export Software Collection
  • Import Collection in the target system

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/6896de5f70c54753ac028f444eafa16f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/74940689b4a54c89a04f11dc2247701a.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3126032

Point 3: Developer Extensibility when analytics isn’t enough

Step 1: Set up the ABAP development environment

  • install ABAP Development Tools (ADT)
  • create an ABAP Cloud project and connect to your system

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e3527dfa1c5a41f69d4e63691f174f0a.html

Step 2: Use only released APIs/objects (clean core)

SAP Developer Extensibility guidance is cloud-safe develop using released extension points/APIs/objects

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/using-developer-in-app-extensibility-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html

To find released APIs:

  • use SAP Business Accelerator Hub

More details below:

https://api.sap.com/

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/1e60f14bdc224c2c975c8fa8bcfd7f3f.html

Step 3: Build the report app using RAP

RAP is the SAP-recommended model for modern ABAP applications (incl. Public Cloud)

More details from SAP Community:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/abap/rap

Step 4: Publish + authorize + transport

Point 4. Best practices:

  • Start from standard (View/Query Browser) and only build what’s missing
  • Prefer Key User analytics first it’s faster, upgrade-safe and intended for reporting
  • For ABAP, stick to released APIs/objects (clean core) to stay upgrade-safe
  • Design for performance keep joins minimal, always test with realistic volumes and enforce filters/parameters early
  • Treat publish to users as part of the deliverable tile + catalog extension + role assignment

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL Appliance shows error when activating
entry.content:
Hi Prakash_Govindaraj1,

The message you received indicates that the free trial period for the appliance template you are using has expired. To continue using the appliance beyond the trial, please ensure the following prerequisites are met:

- To own SAP Product Licenses for the required appliance template.
- To purchase the SAP Cloud Appliance Library subscription package.

Please note that the free trial period begins when you accept the template’s terms and conditions.

For more information, see https://cal.sap.com/catalog#/support -> Business Information for Appliance Templates and SAP Licenses Information for Appliance Templates sections.

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Not able to derive material Basic Data - Size/dimensions
entry.content:
Hello @Chandra_S ,

This behaviour is not expected. Of course USMD_RULE_SERVICE_CROSS_ET BAdI requires changed data to be triggered but you don't need to enter a value in Size/Dimension particularly to derive this field. I think there is some logic/overwrite issue in code.

I suggest you to analyse the implementation of USMD_RULE_SERVICE_CROSS_ET BAdI, also you might want to check the implementation in USMD_RULE_SERVICE BAdI. This BAdI imp may also overwrite this field at runtime.

For more information about derivations for material, you can check this link.

entry.title: Re: Record quantity indicator in center cost

entry.title: Re: KE30.
entry.content:
KE30 (Profitability Report) only picks up values that flow through CO‑PA value fields. Direct postings from FI/MM (like FB50 or MIRO) don’t automatically appear unless they are transferred into CO‑PA via the correct account assignment and valuation logic.

How to Fix

Check PA Transfer Structure (KEI1/KEI2): Ensure the cost elements used in FI/MM postings are mapped to the correct value fields.

Check Account Assignment: Make sure the FI/MM postings carry a profitability segment (e.g., customer, product). Without this, CO‑PA won’t record the values.

Validate Costing Keys: Confirm that the cost classes assigned to cost groups are correctly tied to the intended value fields.

Run Reconciliation (KEAT): Compare FI/MM postings with CO‑PA to see which postings are missing and why.

entry.title: BW Modernisation with the SAP BW Data Product Generator
entry.content:
The DataProduct Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit

With the "SAP BW/4HANA DataProduct Generator for SAP BDC" a functionality is availabe as part of the BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit to expose existing SAP BW/4HANA artifacts ( i.e. Advanced DataStore Objects, CompositeProviders and Queries as an InfoProvider) as local tables to the Object Store. For SAP BW 7.5 there is a SAP GUI based UI available. In a second step the Data Sharing Cockpit of Datasphere can be used to create DataProducts out of such local tables.

1) Activities in the Data Provisioning Cockpit:

Scenario: The underlying Scenario of this chapter is to create a DataProduct out of an Advanced DataStore Object from a SAP BW/4HANA system in the private Cloud which containes additional CashFlow data. This data should be made available as a DataProduct to BDC to be merged with the existing Working Capital data from the exercises before.

Update March 2026: Since the availability of SAP BW/4HANA Modeling Tools in Eclipse 1.27 there is an additional way to move a BW/4HANA InfoProvider to the Object Store of SAP Datasphere (as a basis for the Data Product generation). As a result, a subscription is generated. A data subscription is a new BW object type that allows you to extract data from ODP-based sources within your BW system into local tables (file) in the SAP Datasphere system of your SAP Business Data Cloud formation. In the following chapters both options are described:

1.1) Exposing an InfoProvider of SAP BW/4HANA via the Data Product Generator (in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit)

  • Establish a subscription in SAP BW/4HANA Administration Cockpit, select the InfoProvider which needs to be exposed to the ObjectStore and activate the subscription:

(remark: "CF in thge description of the objects in the screenshots is referring to the scenario of the exercise and means "Cash Flow")

  • Regular transfers can be scheduled from the subscription using a BW Process Chain. With every execution of the BW Process Chain the data is pushed into the SAP Datasphere object store. There is a new Process Type available to be used to propagate the data of an InfoProvider to the ObjectStore "Load Process and Postprocessing - Data Subscription":

The status of the latest execution of a subscription can be checked in the subscription overview:

For each subscription the local table can be viewed as well as its content. By pressing "View Local Table" for a given subscription you will be guided to the Datasphere instance and the respective local table in the Data Builder. From here the content of the table can also be checked.

1.2) Exposing an InfoProvider of SAP BW/4HANA via the Scenario Generator (with the SAP BW/4HANA Modeling Tools in Eclipse)

With the SAP BW/4HANA Modelling Tools in Eclipse 1.27 there is an additional possibility to expose a BW InfoProvider to the Object Store of SAP Datasphere. Eitherway, regardless which option is used, the local table (subscription) has to be used as a basis in the Data Sharing Cockpit to generate a Data Product.

There are three important concepts which neeed to be considered using the Scenario Generator:

A 'scenario' is a list of references between data subscriptions which are required to reconstruct a specific BW model (such as a DataStore object (advanced) (aDSO) or a CompositeProvider (HCPR)). For example, if you have a CompositeProvider, you would not only need a data subscription to represent the top-level object, but also further data subscriptions for the InfoObjects it contains and other dependencies.

The 'scenario tree' saves these dependencies between the objects in the BW model, as well as the equivalent data subscriptions and their dependencies. It also considers existing data subscriptions for dependent objects to avoid the creation of duplicate data subscriptions and local tables (file).

The 'Scenario Generator' lets you not only create scenarios out of your BW models, but also mass generate the associated data subscriptions out of the scenario tree. Through this you can replicate an entire BW model into local tables (file) in your SAP Datasphere tenant in SAP Business Data Cloud. There, the extracted data and associated semantics are stored in table format and become available for further use cases, such as creating data products or sharing to SAP Databricks.

So the basic workflow in the Scenario Generator is:

a) To create a Scenario which is a collection of all Objects belonging to a given BW InfoProvider and their dependencies. For example a CompositeProvider alone does not contain any data. The data is coming from its PartProviders so a scenario of a CompositeProvider would also include the PartProviders.

b) Generate the subscriptions which consists of the lokal tables and the mechanisms to replicate the data from the InfoProviders to the local tables

As a basis for the Data Product to be created this time the CompositeProvider (DPG_CP_CF) from the scenario above is taken. In the Scenario Generators first tab 'Scenario Core Objects' some general settings can be made before collecting the objects related to a selected InfoProvider to create the scenario and the dependency tree of all objects belonging to the scenario.

  • 'Selection Grouping' defines which related objects shoud be collected for a selected BW InfoProvider. It can be specified that 'only necessary' objects are taken into consideration or also the Upstream and downstream objects along the Dataflow of the InfoProvider.
  • The 'Association Levels' define the amount of levels included in the dependency tree.
  • There are some optional checkboxes to activate the possibility to transport subscriptions or to consider already existing subscriptions to prevent duplicates and redundant subscriptions on objects where a subscription already exists.

After pressing the 'Collect' Button, all dependent objects from the CompositeProvider are collected which are the Inbound ADSO as the only PartProvider and the second Object collected is the InfoObject 'Company Code' which is the only real InfoObject in the Inbound ADSO (rest of the columns are fieldbased).

After the collection of the objects the relationsships are displayed in the Tab 'Object Relations'. In this list all subscriptions which are generated in this second step can be seen and the generation of the subscription triggered. After this the 'Message' tab is shown where all relevant messages from the generation process can be seen.

2) Creation of a DataProduct out of the exposed SAP BW/4HANA ADSO

To create a DataProduct from the local table the Data Sharing Studio from Datasphere must be used. In a BDC Formation there can only be one Datasphere System assigned to the BW 7.5 or BW/4HANA instance.

Before the DataProduct can be generated, a Data Provider Profile must be defined (or an existing one used) where the mandatory flag "Data Provider Visibility" is set to "Unified Customer Landscape Formations".

Now a new DataProduct can be created (Data Sharing Cockpit > My Data Products > Create Product)

In the definition of the DataProduct the fields for the Contexts and Deliverymode are automatically taken from the Data Provider Profile and to connect to the right ADSO the corresponding Datasphere Space needs to be specified where the ADSO is located:

After saving the DataProduct it should be available in the overview table under "My Data Products". By selecting this entry in the table the Status can be switched to "listed".

3) Next Steps

Optional: create a graphical view to bring the data of this newly created DataProduct together with the Working Capital / Chash Flow data which is already available from the exercises before.

You can also now continue with the reading material that is provided about accessing Data products from customized S/4HANA CDS views.

4) Important Links / Notes

Pre-requisites

  • SAP Note [3590400 - Configuration SAP BW and SAP BW/4HANA: Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3590400)

  • SAP Note [3590318 Pilot BW Configuration for Data Product Generator in SAP BDC] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3590318)

  • SAP Note [3593519, TCI2 for BW on HANA, BW/4HANA] (https://me.sap.com/notes/3593519)

  • SAP Note 3581186 - OA2C: RFC API

  • SAP Notes for Transaction SOAUTH2_CLIENT:

  • 3532137 - OA2C: Dynpro UI for OAuth2.0 Client Configuration

  • 3561719 - OA2C: New dynpro UI SOAUTH2_CLIENT, generation of DDIC object

  • 3561640 - OA2C: Token Status for SOAUTH2_CLIENT

  • 3692414 - Data Subscription TLOGO: After TCI 3.0

  • Supported Releases of SAP BW 7.5 / SAP BW/4HANA:

  • SAP BW 7.50 SP24 onwards

  • SAP BW/4HANA 2021 SP4 onwards

  • SAP BW/4HANA 2023 SP0 onwards
